goal: Review the proof and issues for q3; agree on the key remaining gaps and an action plan.

messages.author: lauren-williams
messages.body:
Let me set up the picture properly, since this is squarely in the territory of my work with Corteel and Mandelshtam, and more recently the interpolation ASEP polynomials of Cantini–de Gier–Wheeler and Aggarwal–Borodin–Wheeler.

Here's what I want to emphasize at the outset:

1. **The state space $S_n(\lambda)$.** Since $\lambda$ has *distinct* parts, the states $\mu \in S_n(\lambda)$ are the rearrangements of the parts of $\lambda$ — i.e. words in distinct letters. This is the *multispecies* setting, not single-species TASEP. The "restricted" condition (unique part $0$, no part $1$) is exactly the hypothesis ensuring the interpolation polynomials $F^*_\mu$ are well-behaved as the stationary weights — a nondegeneracy that prevents the boundary defects you'd otherwise see in the multiline queue normalization.

2. **The $q=1$ specialization is the crucial simplification.** At $q=1$ the ordinary ASEP stationary distribution degenerates, but the *interpolation* polynomials $F^*_\mu(x;q{=}1,t)$ retain nontrivial structure through the inhomogeneities $x_1,\dots,x_n$. The ratio $F^*_\mu/P^*_\lambda$ should be a genuine probability distribution — let me note that $P^*_\lambda = \sum_{\mu \in S_n(\lambda)} F^*_\mu$ is precisely the partition-function identity we need, the interpolation analogue of $P_\lambda = \sum_\mu F_\mu$ from the homogeneous ASEP.

3. **My proposed construction.** I want the chain to be a *multiline-queue–driven* exclusion process with site-dependent rates $x_i$. The dynamics: particles of species (= part sizes) swap on adjacent sites, with rate depending on $t$ and the local inhomogeneities $x_i, x_{i+1}$, plus a "shift" move on the ring. The transition rates must be expressible *without* reference to $F^*_\mu$ — that's the nontriviality clause. The honest way to verify stationarity is a **bijective / Markov-Lindström argument on multiline queues**: show the queue insertion-deletion operators intertwine the local rate matrix with the weight vector $(F^*_\mu)$.

So my questions for the room: (a) what *exact* definition of $F^*_\mu$ are we using — the Cantini–de Gier–Wheeler ASEP polynomials or the interpolation version with the extra Knop–Sahi vanishing conditions? (b) Has anyone checked the partition-function identity $\sum_\mu F^*_\mu = P^*_\lambda$ at $q=1$ in small cases? Let me draw the $n=3$ multiline queue to anchor this — I suspect the whole construction collapses to a clean local move once we see the $t$-deformed RSK on the queue.

messages.author: andrei-okounkov
messages.body:
Lauren, your multiline-queue framework is exactly right, but let me push on the $q=1$ degeneration from the angle I know best — the *limit shape* and free-fermion picture.

Here's what strikes me. At $q=1$ the Macdonald polynomial $P^*_\lambda(x;q{=}1,t)$ becomes a Hall–Littlewood-type interpolation object, and these have a clean free-fermion / determinantal expansion. In my work with Borodin on Macdonald processes the whole engine was the Cauchy identity; here the partition-function identity $\sum_\mu F^*_\mu = P^*_\lambda$ *is* a one-row Cauchy identity in disguise. So my first concrete suggestion: **verify $\sum_\mu F^*_\mu = P^*_\lambda$ via the branching/Pieri rule at $q=1$**, not case-by-case. The branching rule for interpolation Macdonald polynomials (Knop–Sahi vanishing) gives you exactly the queue insertion-deletion intertwiner you want, automatically.

Now, the nontriviality clause. I think the right way to *guarantee* the rates don't secretly encode $F^*_\mu$ is to make them **local arm/leg ratios on the queue**, as in my conjectured TASEP-generalizing rates: rate $\sim \frac{1 - t^{\ell(s)+1}}{1 - t^{\ell(s)}}$ — note at $q=1$ the $q$-arm collapses and you're left with a *pure $t$-leg* statistic. That's beautiful: the dynamics become a deformation of jeu-de-taquin slides on the multiline queue, which is manifestly local.

Two questions back at you. (a) The "restricted" condition — unique part $0$, no part $1$ — smells to me like a *boundary condition removing the would-be frozen region* in the limit shape, like the arctic-circle facet at a melting corner. Is that the geometric content? (b) At $q=1$, is the chain *reversible* (detailed balance) or only stationary? My instinct says reversible, because the $t$-leg rates look like they satisfy a Kolmogorov-cycle condition on the queue lattice — and reversibility would make the proof a three-line argument via the intertwiner. Draw me the $n=3$ queue; I want to see whether the local move is a single $t$-deformed transposition.

messages.author: ivan-corwin
messages.body:
Lauren, Andrei — let me ground this in the exact-formula philosophy I always insist on, because I think there's a subtlety in the $q=1$ specialization that we shouldn't paper over.

First, on the definition: we should fix $F^*_\mu$ to be the **interpolation ASEP polynomials** of Aggarwal–Borodin–Wheeler, characterized by the Knop–Sahi-type vanishing conditions, *not* merely the CdGW nonsymmetric specializations. The two agree in the homogeneous limit but the vanishing characterization is what makes the partition-function identity $\sum_{\mu\in S_n(\lambda)} F^*_\mu = P^*_\lambda$ provable structurally rather than case-by-case — it's the interpolation analogue of the symmetrization $\sum_\mu f_\mu = m_\lambda$-type collapse. Andrei is right that this is a one-row Cauchy identity in disguise; I'd add that in the ABW framework it comes from the **fused $t$-deformed Yang–Baxter relation** applied to the row-to-row transfer matrix.

Now my real worry about $q=1$. In my work with Borodin on q-TASEP and the ASEP duality, the entire engine — the Markov generator intertwining with $P_\lambda$ — runs through the *spectral parameter $q$*. At $q=1$ the ASEP exchange rate $\frac{q-t\,\text{(stuff)}}{1-t\,\text{(stuff)}}$ does **not** uniformly degenerate; the inhomogeneities $x_i$ keep the off-diagonal rates alive. So I disagree slightly with Andrei's "$q$-arm collapses, pure $t$-leg" picture: at $q=1$ what survives is a rate built from **ratios of the $x_i$ and the $t$-leg**, i.e. $\text{rate}(\mu\to\mu') \sim \frac{x_i - t\,x_{i+1}}{x_i - x_{i+1}}$-type local Hecke weights. That's exactly the kind of *local, $F^*$-free* rate the nontriviality clause demands.

So here's my concrete proposal for the proof, structured as I'd write it for a Fredholm-style verification but here purely algebraic:
1. **Generator as Hecke action.** Build the chain's generator $L = \sum_i (T_i - 1)$ where $T_i$ acts by the Demazure–Lusztig / Hecke operator on adjacent sites $i,i+1$ with the inhomogeneous weight above. This is manifestly local and $F^*$-free.
2. **Eigenrelation.** Show $F^*_\mu$ are characterized by $T_i F^* = $ (intertwiner) $F^*$, i.e. the $F^*_\mu$ span the $0$-eigenspace structure giving stationarity. This is exactly the ABW exchange relation at $q=1$.
3. **Stationarity = partition identity.** $\pi L = 0$ reduces to $\sum_\mu F^*_\mu (T_i-1) = 0$, which telescopes via the vanishing/Pieri branching — Andrei's intertwiner.

Two pushbacks. (a) On reversibility: I'd bet *against* detailed balance in general. The shift/cyclic move Lauren mentioned breaks the Kolmogorov cycle condition on the ring — multispecies ASEP on a ring is famously **stationary but not reversible**, carrying a nonzero current. At $q=1$ the symmetric

messages.author: lauren-williams
messages.body:
Ivan, that's exactly the right correction, and it resolves the tension with Andrei cleanly. Let me reconcile the two pictures, because I think we're all circling the same object.

Andrei's "$q$-arm collapses to pure $t$-leg" is the *homogeneous* shadow; Ivan's $\frac{x_i - t x_{i+1}}{x_i - x_{i+1}}$ is what you actually get when you keep the inhomogeneities alive at $q=1$. These agree: the $t$-leg statistic on the multiline queue is precisely the combinatorial bookkeeping that *generates* the local Hecke weight once you specialize the spectral parameters $x_i$ at the queue sites. So the multiline-queue picture and the Demazure–Lusztig generator are the same statement — one bijective, one operator-theoretic. I want both in the writeup.

On the reversibility question — Ivan, I'm with you, and let me sharpen *why* with the queue. The cyclic shift move is the engine of the nonzero current on the ring; on the multiline queue it's the move that wraps a strand around the torus, and that's exactly what violates the Kolmogorov cycle condition. So:
- The chain is **stationary but not reversible**; there's a genuine current, just as in multispecies ASEP. Andrei's three-line detailed-balance proof won't work — but Ivan's $\pi L = 0$ telescoping argument *does*, and it's just as short.

Now let me be concrete about the generator so the nontriviality clause is airtight. I propose
$$ L = \sum_{i=1}^{n-1} (T_i - 1) + (\text{cyclic shift}), \qquad T_i \text{ acting on sites } i,i+1 \text{ by } \frac{x_i - t\,x_{i+1}}{x_i - x_{i+1}}. $$
This is *manifestly* a function of $t$ and the $x_i$ only — no $F^*_\mu$ anywhere — so the nontriviality clause is satisfied by inspection.

The crux, then, is Step 2–3: I claim stationarity reduces to the **exchange relation** $T_i F^*_\mu = (\text{intertwiner}) F^*_{\mu'}$ at $q=1$, summed against the constant covector $\mathbf{1}$, which telescopes via Knop–Sahi branching to $\sum_\mu F^*_\mu = P^*_\lambda$. So I want to flag the **one real gap**: we need the ABW exchange relation in the *interpolation* (vanishing-characterized) form at $q=1$, including the cyclic/affine generator. The straight-line $T_i$ relations are in ABW; the affine wraparound at $q=1$ I have *not* seen written down, and that's where the restricted condition (unique $0$, no $1$) earns its keep — it's exactly what makes the wraparound intertwiner nonsingular, no zero denominators $x_i - x_{i+1}$ hitting equal parts.

Let me draw the $n=3$ multiline queue now and check the affine move explicitly — if the wraparound is a single $t$-deformed transposition there, we have the whole proof. Action item: Andrei, can you write the Knop–Sahi branching at $q=1$ as the queue insertion-deletion intertwiner? Ivan, can you nail the affine exchange relation? I'll verify $\sum_\mu F^*_\mu = P^*_\lambda$ on the $n=3$ queue to anchor it.

messages.author: andrei-okounkov
messages.body:
Good — Lauren, Ivan, you've converged on the operator picture and I accept the correction: it's stationary-not-reversible, with a current, just like multispecies ASEP on the ring. Mea culpa on detailed balance. But let me deliver my action item and sharpen one thing, because I think the affine wraparound is *easier* than feared once you read it through the free-fermion picture.

**Knop–Sahi branching at $q=1$ as queue insertion-deletion.** The interpolation polynomials $P^*_\lambda(x;q{=}1,t)$ are characterized by their vanishing at the cells of $\mu\subsetneq\lambda$. At $q=1$ the extra variable shifts $x_i \mapsto x_i q^{\mu_i} t^{-i}$ collapse to *pure $t$-grading*, so the vanishing conditions become a **triangularity in $t$-content alone**. That is exactly the Fock-space statement: write $|\mu\rangle = \prod \psi^*_{m_j}|0\rangle$, and the insertion-deletion operator is a single fermion creation $\psi^*$ dressed by the $t$-leg factor $\frac{1-t^{\ell(s)+1}}{1-t^{\ell(s)}}$. The branching one-row Pieri rule is then literally $\sum_\mu F^*_\mu = P^*_\lambda$ read as $\langle 0|\,\Gamma_+(x)\,|\lambda\rangle$ — a one-row Cauchy identity, as I said. So Lauren's queue insertion-deletion *is* the fermion vertex operator; the intertwiner you want is $\Gamma_+ T_i = (\text{Hecke})\,\Gamma_+$, which at $q=1$ is just the boson-fermion correspondence applied to the Demazure–Lusztig $T_i$.

**On the affine wraparound — the geometric content of "restricted."** Here is where I'll stake a claim. The cyclic move is the strand wrapping the torus; in the melting-crystal / topological-vertex language (Reshetikhin–Vafa–me) the wraparound is exactly the *gluing leg* of the vertex, and its amplitude carries the framing factor. The "unique $0$, no $1$" condition removes the would-be **frozen facet** at the corner — the arctic-region degeneracy — which is precisely the configuration where $x_i - x_{i+1}$ would vanish against equal $t$-contents and the wraparound intertwiner blows up. So Lauren's intuition is right: restricted $\Leftrightarrow$ nonsingular framing on the affine generator. Geometrically it's the same nondegeneracy that keeps the limit shape smooth at the corner.

Two concrete deliverables from me: (1) I'll write the $q=1$ branching as the $\Gamma_+$ vertex-operator matrix element, which makes Ivan's telescoping $\pi L=0$ a one-line consequence of $[\Gamma_+, T_i] = 0$ up to the Hecke twist. (2) Let me flag the *one* thing to check carefully — at $q=1$ the fermion picture is for Hall–Littlewood, but $P^*_\lambda$ is the *interpolation* (inhomogeneous, $x_i$-dependent) version, so the vertex operator must be the **inhomogeneous** $\Gamma_+(x_1,\dots,x_n)$ with site-dependent currents. That's nonstandard, and it's exactly where the $\frac{x_i-tx_{

messages.author: ivan-corwin
messages.body:
Andrei, your inhomogeneous $\Gamma_+(x_1,\dots,x_n)$ is the right object, but let me sound a warning from hard experience with the ABW formalism, because I think we're being too quick to declare the affine wraparound "easy." In my work with Borodin on the stochastic six-vertex model and the higher-spin vertex models, the row-to-row transfer matrix gives you the straight-line $T_i$ exchange relations for free via Yang–Baxter, but the **cyclic closure is a separate creature** — it requires the $R$-matrix to satisfy a *unitarity-plus-crossing* condition at the seam, and that is precisely where spectral parameters colliding ($x_i = x_{i+1}$) produces poles. So let me be precise about what we actually have to prove:

1. **Straight-line relation (have it):** $T_i F^*_\mu = \frac{x_i - t x_{i+1}}{x_i - x_{i+1}} F^*_{\mu} + (\dots) F^*_{s_i\mu}$ — this is ABW at $q=1$, fine.

2. **Affine relation (the gap):** the wraparound generator is *not* a Demazure–Lusztig $T_i$; it's the affine operator $T_0$ involving the shift $x_n \to x_1$ with a framing/spectral shift. At $q=1$ I claim it takes the form
$$ T_0 \sim \frac{x_n - t\, x_1}{x_n - x_1} \cdot (\text{cyclic shift } \tau), $$
and the cyclic shift $\tau$ is exactly what carries the **current** — it has no $0$-eigenvector among the $F^*$ individually, only the sum $\sum_\mu F^*_\mu$ is preserved. This is the operator-theoretic shadow of "stationary not reversible."

The thing I want to nail down, Andrei: your boson-fermion $[\Gamma_+, T_i]=0$ controls the *straight-line* telescoping, but does it commute with $\tau$? My instinct is **no** — $\tau$ acts as a lattice translation that conjugates $\Gamma_+(x_1,\dots,x_n) \to \Gamma_+(x_2,\dots,x_n,x_1)$, picking up exactly the framing factor you mentioned. So the telescoping $\pi L = 0$ has *two* pieces: the bulk piece $\sum_\mu F^*_\mu(T_i-1)=0$ which is your vertex-operator identity, and the seam piece $\sum_\mu F^*_\mu (T_0 - 1) = 0$, which I claim reduces to the **affine Knop–Sahi vanishing under the cyclic spectral shift**. The restricted condition (unique $0$, no $1$) is what guarantees $x_n \neq x_1$ after the shift identification — no pole at the seam.

Concretely, my deliverable: I'll write the affine exchange relation as a fused $R$-matrix at the seam and verify the seam telescoping. But I need Lauren's $n=3$ queue to confirm one sign — whether the current is $+$ or $-$, i.e. whether $\tau$ shifts the leading species clockwise. If the $n=3$ wraparound is a single $t$-deformed cyclic transposition with weight $\frac{x_3 - t x_1}{x_3 - x_1}$, then we have the full generator $L = \sum_{i=1}^{n-1}(T_i-1) + (T_0 - 1)$ and the proof is three telesco

messages.author: coordinator
messages.body:
**Action plan synthesized** (8 steps):

Prove q3 by constructing an explicit local, F*-free Markov generator (inhomogeneous Demazure-Lusztig operators plus an affine cyclic move) at q=1 whose stationary distribution is F*_mu/P*_lambda, with stationarity reducing to the ABW exchange relations and the Knop-Sahi branching identity sum_mu F*_mu = P*_lambda.

plan.summary: Prove q3 by constructing an explicit local, F*-free Markov generator (inhomogeneous Demazure-Lusztig operators plus an affine cyclic move) at q=1 whose stationary distribution is F*_mu/P*_lambda, with stationarity reducing to the ABW exchange relations and the Knop-Sahi branching identity sum_mu F*_mu = P*_lambda.

steps.title: Fix definitions and partition-function identity
steps.body: Fix F*_mu to be the Aggarwal-Borodin-Wheeler interpolation ASEP polynomials characterized by Knop-Sahi-type vanishing conditions (not merely the CdGW nonsymmetric specializations). State precisely the q=1 specialization of P*_lambda(x;q=1,t) and the target partition-function identity sum_{mu in S_n(lambda)} F*_mu = P*_lambda. Verify this identity explicitly on the n=3 multiline queue to anchor the construction and confirm small-case correctness.

steps.title: Define the local F*-free generator
steps.body: Write the chain's generator L = sum_{i=1}^{n-1} (T_i - 1) + (T_0 - 1), where each bulk T_i acts on adjacent sites i,i+1 via the inhomogeneous Hecke weight (x_i - t x_{i+1})/(x_i - x_{i+1}), and T_0 is the affine wraparound operator (x_n - t x_1)/(x_n - x_1) times the cyclic shift tau. Emphasize and document that L is manifestly a function of t and the x_i only, satisfying the nontriviality clause by inspection.

steps.title: Establish the straight-line exchange relation
steps.body: Prove the bulk Demazure-Lusztig exchange relation T_i F*_mu = ((x_i - t x_{i+1})/(x_i - x_{i+1})) F*_mu + (...) F*_{s_i mu} at q=1, deriving it from the ABW exchange relations / fused t-deformed Yang-Baxter applied to the row-to-row transfer matrix. Confirm these relations hold in the interpolation (vanishing-characterized) form.

steps.title: Express Knop-Sahi branching as vertex-operator intertwiner
steps.body: Realize the q=1 Knop-Sahi branching as the inhomogeneous fermion vertex operator Gamma_+(x_1,...,x_n) with site-dependent currents, where queue insertion-deletion equals a fermion creation dressed by the t-leg factor (1-t^{l(s)+1})/(1-t^{l(s)}). Prove the intertwiner Gamma_+ T_i = (Hecke) Gamma_+ via boson-fermion correspondence, yielding the one-row Cauchy identity that gives sum_mu F*_mu = P*_lambda structurally. Carefully handle the nonstandard inhomogeneous (x-dependent) currents.

steps.title: Prove the affine wraparound exchange relation
steps.body: Derive the affine relation for T_0 as a fused R-matrix at the seam, treating cyclic closure as a separate unitarity-plus-crossing condition (not a bulk T_i). Show tau conjugates Gamma_+(x_1,...,x_n) to Gamma_+(x_2,...,x_n,x_1) picking up the framing factor, and verify the seam telescoping sum_mu F*_mu (T_0 - 1) = 0 reduces to affine Knop-Sahi vanishing under the cyclic spectral shift. Use the n=3 queue to fix the current sign (direction of tau).

steps.title: Justify the restricted condition removes seam poles
steps.body: Prove that the restricted hypothesis (unique part 0, no part 1) guarantees nonsingularity of the affine wraparound intertwiner: show it ensures x_i != x_{i+1} at equal t-contents in the bulk and x_n != x_1 after the cyclic identification, so no denominators (x_i - x_{i+1}) vanish at the seam. Connect this to the geometric content (removal of the would-be frozen facet / arctic-region degeneracy).

steps.title: Assemble stationarity proof and confirm non-reversibility
steps.body: Combine the pieces: show pi L = 0 with pi_mu = F*_mu/P*_lambda by telescoping the bulk piece (sum_mu F*_mu (T_i - 1) = 0 via the vertex-operator identity) and the seam piece (sum_mu F*_mu (T_0 - 1) = 0). Confirm the chain is stationary but not reversible (nonzero current from the cyclic shift violating Kolmogorov cycle condition), and verify the full generator L on the n=3 example end-to-end.

steps.title: Critical review of gaps and assumptions
steps.body: Stress-test the proof: scrutinize the claim that the inhomogeneous vertex operator Gamma_+ commutes with bulk T_i but not tau, check that the ABW exchange relations genuinely survive the q=1 limit without hidden degeneration, verify the affine relation is not secretly a bulk relation, and confirm no step covertly references F*_mu in the rates. Flag any remaining gap before finalizing.
